prompt: We are building an AI-powered CV screening tool that ranks job applicants for employers in the EU. Is this high-risk under the EU AI Act? Which Annex III area applies, what deadline do we need to comply by, and what are our main obligations as the provider?
assertions: Classifies the CV screening/ranking tool as HIGH-RISK under Annex III point 4(a) (employment: recruitment or selection, incl. placing targeted job ads, analysing and filtering applications, evaluating candidates)

States the compliance deadline for Annex III standalone high-risk systems as 2 December 2027 (as amended by the Digital Omnibus, adopted June 2026; originally 2 August 2026). Any deadline stated must be 2 Dec 2027, not Aug 2026.

Walks through core provider obligations citing Arts. 9-17 (risk management, data governance, technical documentation, logging, transparency to deployers, human oversight, accuracy/robustness/cybersecurity, QMS)

Mentions conformity assessment and registration: self-assessment per Annex VI for Annex III point 4 systems, EU declaration of conformity (Art. 47), CE marking (Art. 48), EU database registration (Art. 49)
Notes the Art. 6(3) derogation exists but explains it is narrow and does not apply where the system profiles natural persons / materially influences hiring decisions (profiling is always high-risk)
